title: Keep the field Username visible when selecting External Tool
author: Alessio71
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->

## Expected Behavior
<!--- If you're describing a bug, tell us what should happen -->
<!--- If you're suggesting a change/improvement, tell us how it should work -->
In previous versions the field Username was always visible, even when changing Protocol to External Tool

## Current Behavior
<!--- If describing a bug, tell us what happens instead of the expected behavior -->
<!--- If suggesting a change/improvement, exp...

iterations.description: The 'Username' field (AuthenticationUsernameTextBox) becomes invisible, likely due to a UI rendering or sizing issue within the TableLayoutPanel or its parent container, as no explicit hiding logic is found in the code-behind.
Bulk verified: commit dae524af exists (HIGH confidence)

comments.author: Alessio71
comments.snippet:
Username appeared even when External tool is selected, issue fixed. thank you.
Will the password without focus by default be address too?
Thank you very much for your help.
Alex

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: I think We fix autofocus, could you check last NB please?

comments.author: Alessio71
comments.snippet:
On build 3208 is not working
Thanks,
*Alessio*

comments.author: Alessio71
comments.snippet:
> I think We fix autofocus, could you check last NB please?
@Kvarkas,
autofocus Is still not working on opening on 1.78.2.3228, after the splash screen disappears, the unlock password request has no focus.
Wasn't working on Windows 11 x64 24H2, still not working now on 25H2.
Is not a big issue anyway, all the rest is working just fine
Thank you for your work,
Alessio

comments.author: Kvarkas

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

comments.author: Alessio71
comments.snippet:
I've tried Nightly Build — 20260225 (v1.81.0-beta.4 a bit better, I was launching my external tools with .\ prefix to the command; removing it now they launch correctly, only the weird tab remains but when you close it now disappears without errors (maybe because it managed to launch the external tool correctly).
The focus on password request after logo works better too, doesn't get the focus only when launched from the taskbar pinned app.
Thank you!

comments.author: Alessio71
comments.snippet: I have to correct what I wrote, the launching of external tools works only once, every time I have to close the weird tab to make it work again (it's  a bit frustrating)

comments.author: Alessio71
comments.snippet: BTW, this tab and then this when you close it, appears even clicking once on the Connection entry It appears even with connection that are using native RDP connection (not only the one with External tool)

comments.author: Alessio71
comments.snippet:
I'm using v1.81.0-beta.4, password is now visible when external tool is selected, I confirm that that issue is fixed.
Remains the (minor) focus on password issue when mRemoteNG is launched as a pined app from the taskbar (the focus is working when launched from a link from the desktop, for example) and the new issue about the annoying tab that opens when clicking on every connection and that prevents the connection if you don't close it and re double click the (same) connection once more.
Thank ...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The 'Username' field (AuthenticationUsernameTextBox) becomes invisible, likely due to a UI rendering or sizing issue within the TableLayoutPanel or its parent container, as no explicit hiding logic is found in the code-behind.
Approach: Adjust the minimum height of the 'PropertiesGroupBox' and TableLayoutPanel to ensure sufficient space for all authentication-related controls, preventing them from being clipped or pushed out of view.
